title: Ozempic Is Doing Something to the Brain That Nobody Prescribed It For
description: A 95,000-person Swedish cohort study found semaglutide associated with 42–47% reductions in depression, anxiety, and substance use disorder severity — numbers that rival dedicated psychiatric medications. The drug wasn't designed for any of this. The question now isn't whether it works on the brain. It's why, and for whom, and what the pharmacovigilance signals warning of suicidality mean for the people caught in between.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Ozempic Is Doing Something to the Brain That Nobody Prescribed It For
